source_docs.title: ISSAAC-seq enables sensitive and flexible multimodal profiling of chromatin accessibility and gene expression in single cells

oligos.name: ME_S5 (Nextera S5 / Read 1 transposon adapter, top strand)
oligos.role: Transferred strand of the Tn5 S5 (Nextera Read 1) transposon adapter; annealed to ME_Bottom to load Tn5
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera S5 tagmentation oligo. Dissolved to 100 uM and annealed with ME_Bottom to form S5_adaptor.

oligos.name: ME_S7 (Nextera S7 / Read 2 transposon adapter, top strand)
oligos.role: Transferred strand of the Tn5 S7 (Nextera Read 2) transposon adapter; annealed to ME_Bottom to load Tn5
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera S7 tagmentation oligo. Annealed with ME_Bottom to form S7_adaptor.

oligos.name: ME_Bottom (non-transferred mosaic-end strand)
oligos.role: Phosphorylated bottom (non-transferred) mosaic-end strand common to both S5 and S7 transposome adapters
oligos.notes: 5' phosphorylated (/5Phos/) in the protocol; reverse complement of the mosaic end AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG.

oligos.name: S5_adaptor (Tn5 S5 loaded adapter)
oligos.role: Double-stranded Nextera S5 adapter (ME_S5 annealed to ME_Bottom) loaded onto Tn5 to make Tn5-S5/S7 and Tn5-S5/S5 transposomes
oligos.notes: Annealed 98 C to 16 C ramp; used at 20 uM in transposome assembly.

oligos.name: S7_adaptor (Tn5 S7 loaded adapter)
oligos.role: Double-stranded Nextera S7 adapter (ME_S7 annealed to ME_Bottom) loaded onto Tn5 to make Tn5-S5/S7 and Tn5-S7/S7 transposomes

oligos.name: TruseqR1_oligo_dT (in situ RT primer, plate/FACS workflow)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer: partial TruSeq Read 1 handle + 10-bp UMI + poly(dT); primes mRNA in the nucleus with Maxima H- reverse transcriptase

components.name: Poly(dT)
components.role: anchors to mRNA poly-A tail; exact T count per Supplementary Table 4

oligos.notes: Used at 2 uM in the plate (FACS) RNA workflow. The paper states a partial TruSeq adapter, a 10-bp UMI and poly-T; exact poly(dT) length/anchor are in Supplementary Table 4 (not reproduced here).

oligos.name: TruseqR2_oligo_dT (in situ RT primer, 10x droplet workflow)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer variant used in the 10x droplet workflow: partial TruSeq Read 2 handle + 10-bp UMI + poly(dT)
oligos.notes: Droplet-workflow analog of TruseqR1_oligo_dT (not used in the FACS library shown here). Exact sequence per Supplementary Table 4.

oligos.name: N7xx primer (Nextera i7 well-barcode primer)
oligos.role: Adds the per-well i7 cell barcode and the P7-side handle to the Nextera S7 end during in-well pre-amplification

components.name: Nextera S7 (Read 2) handle
components.role: anneals to the S7 transposon end

oligos.notes: Present at 10 uM in the 384-well lysis buffer; each well carries a unique i7 index (scATAC i7 index plate). Read as Index 1.

oligos.name: Truseq_S5_short primer
oligos.role: Extends the RT-primer TruSeq Read 1 stub to the full TruSeq Read 1 handle on the RNA library during pre-amplification
oligos.notes: Full Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer; supplied at 10 uM in the lysis buffer.

oligos.name: Nextra_S5_short primer
oligos.role: Nextera S5 (Read 1) short handle primer added during pre-amplification to prime the S5 end of the ATAC (and ATAC+RNA) library
oligos.notes: Nextera Read 1 / S5 handle; supplied at 10 uM. Added in the ATAC+RNA pre-PCR step.

oligos.name: RNA_plate_S5xx primer (final RNA library i5/plate-barcode primer)
oligos.role: Adds P5 and the per-plate i5 barcode onto the TruSeq Read 1 end of the RNA gene-expression library

oligos.name: ATAC_plate_S5xx primer (final ATAC library i5/plate-barcode primer)
oligos.role: Adds P5 and the per-plate i5 barcode onto the Nextera S5 end of the ATAC library

components.name: Nextera S5 (Read 1) handle
components.role: anneals to the S5 transposon end

oligos.name: Illumina P5 primer
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell primer used in final library amplification

oligos.name: Illumina P7 primer
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell primer used in final library amplification

annotation_lines: ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG = reverse complement of Illumina P7 adapter

library_generation.title: Tn5 transposome assembly
library_generation.summary: Anneal ME_S5/ME_S7 to ME_Bottom and load onto Tn5 to build Tn5-S5/S7, Tn5-S7/S7 and Tn5-S5/S5 transposomes.
library_generation.note: ME_S5 or ME_S7 (100 uM) is annealed to the phosphorylated ME_Bottom (98 C -> 16 C) to form the S5_adaptor and S7_adaptor duplexes, then mixed with purified Tn5.
library_generation.product:
S5_adaptor:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
3'-               TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5'

S7_adaptor:
5'- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
3'-                TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5'

library_generation.title: In situ chromatin tagmentation (ATAC)
library_generation.summary: Tn5-S5/S7 tagments open chromatin in intact nuclei at 30 C, inserting Nextera S5 and S7 adapters.
library_generation.note: Open chromatin is fragmented and both ends receive a mosaic end plus an S5 or S7 handle, leaving a 9-bp gap on the non-transferred strand.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG XXXX...XXXX          CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT -3'
3'-               TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC XXXX...XXXX GAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG -5'

library_generation.title: In situ reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: TruseqR1_oligo_dT primes mRNA and Maxima H- RT synthesizes first-strand cDNA in the nucleus.
library_generation.note: The RT primer contributes a partial TruSeq Read 1 handle and a 10-bp UMI at the 5' end of the cDNA; poly(dT) anneals to the transcript poly-A tail. PEG8000 crowding boosts efficiency.

library_generation.title: RNA-DNA hybrid tagmentation (SHERRY)
library_generation.summary: A Tn5-S7/S7 homodimer tags the RNA-DNA hybrid, adding a mosaic end + S7 handle to the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.note: The RNA/DNA hybrid is directly tagmented, appending the Nextera S7 (Read 2) adapter without full-length cDNA amplification or template switching.

library_generation.title: EXO I digestion and gap fill-in
library_generation.summary: Thermolabile Exonuclease I removes excess primers and Maxima H- fills the 9-bp mosaic-end gaps to make fully duplex, adapter-flanked fragments.
library_generation.note: Applied in situ at 37 C to both ATAC and RNA-hybrid fragments before single-nucleus isolation.

library_generation.title: FACS single-nucleus sorting into barcoded lysis plate
library_generation.summary: DAPI+ single nuclei are sorted into 384-well plates whose lysis buffer carries a unique i7 (N7xx) well barcode plus Truseq_S5_short and Nextra_S5_short primers.
library_generation.note: Each well is defined by a unique Nextera N7xx i7 index; SDS lysis releases the in-situ-processed fragments.
library_generation.product:
Well contains: N7xx (P7-[i7:8]-S7 handle), Truseq_S5_short (full TruSeq R1), Nextra_S5_short (S5 handle)
+ in-situ RNA-hybrid and ATAC fragments from one nucleus.

library_generation.title: Pre-amplification / well-barcode addition
library_generation.summary: Gap fill-in (72 C) then PCR with N7xx + Truseq_S5_short (RNA) and N7xx + Nextra_S5_short (ATAC) adds the per-well i7 barcode and completes the read handles.
library_generation.note: RNA fragments gain P7-i7-S7 on the Nextera end and the full TruSeq Read 1 on the RT-primer end; ATAC fragments gain P7-i7-S7 and the S5 handle.

library_generation.title: Pooling, purification and EXO I cleanup
library_generation.summary: All wells of a plate are pooled, column-purified, and residual primers are removed with thermolabile EXO I before final PCR.
library_generation.note: Plates are kept separate so a per-plate i5 barcode can be added next.
library_generation.product: Pooled per-plate RNA + ATAC pre-libraries (i7 well barcodes set; i5 plate barcode not yet added).

library_generation.title: Final library amplification and plate-barcode addition
library_generation.summary: PCR with RNA_plate_S5xx + ATAC_plate_S5xx + Illumina P7 adds P5 and the per-plate i5 barcode, yielding separate RNA (gene-expression) and ATAC libraries.
library_generation.note: The RNA library carries P5-i5-TruSeq Read 1 on one end and S7-i7-P7 on the other; the ATAC library carries P5-i5-Nextera S5 and S7-i7-P7.

library_sequencing.note: Primes on the P5/TruSeq (i5) end, annealing to the bottom strand; the first 10 nt read the UMI, the remainder reads into the cDNA insert (through the poly-T anchor).
Primes at the Nextera S7 (N7xx) end on the top strand and reads the 8-bp per-well i7 cell barcode off the bottom-strand template.

On the NextSeq 500 / NovaSeq 6000 the 8-bp per-plate i5 barcode is read from the top-strand template at the P5 end; Index 1 (i7 well) + Index 2 (i5 plate) together form the combinatorial cell barcode.
Primes on the Nextera S7 end, annealing to the top strand, and reads the cDNA insert from the Nextera side (opposite end from Read 1).
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...[cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:8]... -3'
3'- ...[cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:8]... -5'
    <--------GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG
             Read 2 primer (Nextera S7): 5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3', reads leftward into cDNA

title: ISSAAC-seq (FACS)
description: ISSAAC-seq (in situ sequencing hetero RNA–DNA-hybrid after ATAC-seq) is a plate-based (FACS) single-nucleus multi-omics method that jointly profiles chromatin accessibility and gene expression from the same nucleus. In situ, open chromatin is first tagmented by a Tn5-S5/S7 transposome (Nextera adapters); mRNA is then reverse-transcribed in the nucleus with an oligo-dT primer carrying a partial TruSeq Read 1 handle and a 10-bp UMI; the resulting RNA–DNA hybrid is marked by a Tn5-S7/S7 homodimer (SHERRY chemistry). Single DAPI+ nuclei are FACS-sorted into a 384-well plate where a per-well i7 (Nextera N7xx) barcode is added, then a per-plate i5 barcode and P5/P7 are appended. The pool is split into an ATAC library (Nextera S5+S7 ends) and an RNA gene-expression library (TruSeq Read 1 + Nextera S7 ends). Read 1 reads the UMI followed by cDNA, Read 2 reads cDNA from the Nextera side, and the combinatorial cell barcode is formed from Index 1 (i7 well) + Index 2 (i5 plate).

reference.label: ISSAAC-seq enables sensitive and flexible multimodal profiling of chromatin accessibility and gene expression in single cells

original_publication.title: ISSAAC-seq enables sensitive and flexible multimodal profiling of chromatin accessibility and gene expression in single cells

throughput.summary: Plate-based (FACS) workflow for limited cell numbers (384-well); >10,000 nuclei jointly profiled from mouse cortex across FACS + droplet. FACS doublet rate 4.2%.
throughput.cells: 384 wells per plate (FACS); 10,378 mouse-cortex nuclei recovered overall
throughput.rna: Medians >17,000 total UMIs and >4,000 detected genes (E14 mESC, 30 C tagmentation)
throughput.dna: Medians ~16,464 unique reads in peaks (ATAC, FACS mouse cortex)

publication.statistical_model: UMI-based digital counting quantified with STARsolo; ATAC processed with Cell Ranger ATAC / chromap; clustering by modularity-based community detection (Seurat/Signac).

other.label: FACS sequencing recipe
other.value: 75 x 8 x 8 x 75 cycles (Read1 x i7 x i5 x Read2) on NextSeq 500 / NovaSeq 6000

other.label: Key optimization
other.value: Chromatin tagging at 30 C (not 37 C) increases RNA library quality 5-7x without harming ATAC complexity

data_processing.summary: Plate-based (FACS) reads are demultiplexed by sample, then for the RNA modality Index 1 (i7), Index 2 (i5) and the first 10 bp of Read 1 are concatenated into a cell-barcode+UMI FASTQ and quantified against the genome with STARsolo; the paired ATAC reads (Read 1 + Read 2) are aligned with BWA inside a Snakemake pipeline. Downstream, RNA is normalized and clustered in Seurat and ATAC in Signac (Cell Ranger ATAC aggr + MACS2 peaks), and cell types are assigned by label transfer from an Allen Brain scRNA-seq reference.
data_processing.statistical_model: UMI-based digital gene-expression counting (STARsolo); no parametric count distribution specified
